value: Harbin Urban Forest Park (also commonly named Heilongjiang Forest Botanical Garden
units: Chinese: Haerbin Chengshi Senlin Gongyuan / Heilongjiang Sheng Senlin Zhiwuyuan) is a 136 ha forest park located in Xiangfang District of Harbin city. It is the only national-level forest park situated inside the built urban area of a Chinese city (designated 1992) and was originally established in 1958 as a forestry research base. The park is predominantly a planted conifer stand of Pinus sylvestris var. mongolica and Larix gmelinii that is gradually transitioning toward mixed native broadleaf-conifer forest as cohorts age. The cold continental climate (mean annual temperature 3.5 degC; January average -18 degC) limits the effective management window to approximately 5 months per year and suppresses invasive species pressure. Limited municipal funding relative to southern China cities constrains management capacity. A key long-term management goal is facilitating succession from monoculture plantation to diverse native mixed forest within an urban setting.
